title: crotalaria spinosa subsp. aculeata
intro: Crotalaria spinosa subsp. aculeata, also known as the thorny rattlebox, is a plant species within the Crotalaria genus, characterized by its thorny stems and small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Crotalaria spinosa subsp. aculeata include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which are made by drying the plant's leaves and flowers and then steeping them in hot water or applying them directly to affected areas.
